notes: Header/body are the transmission card text. radius_m is the geofence trigger radius. Superchargers and overnight stays use a tight radius since the car is stationary; drive-by and roadside landmarks use a wider radius so the card fires while passing at speed. Headers use ' // ' as the delimiter to fit the cyberdeck terminal look.

landmarks.name: Springfield, MO (Park Central Square)
landmarks.body: Birthplace of Route 66. The road got its number right here in 1926. Every Mother Road run starts on home ground. Yours included.

landmarks.name: Joplin, MO
landmarks.body: Last Missouri town before the line. Mining boomtown and onetime Bonnie and Clyde hideout, now just a hard turn onto the open road west.

landmarks.name: Cars on the Route, Galena, KS
landmarks.body: Kansas gets just thirteen miles of Route 66, and the rusted boom truck here is the one that inspired Tow Mater. Blink and you miss the whole state.

landmarks.name: Coleman Theatre, Miami, OK
landmarks.body: The Coleman Theatre, a 1929 Spanish Revival movie palace dropped into small-town Oklahoma. Gold leaf, a mighty Wurlitzer, and no business being this grand.

landmarks.name: Blue Whale of Catoosa, OK
landmarks.body: A concrete blue whale beached itself in a roadside swimming hole in 1972 and never left. Built as an anniversary gift. The friendliest leviathan on the Mother Road.

landmarks.name: Tesla Supercharger - Tulsa, OK (E 41st St)
landmarks.body: Topping up in the city Cyrus Avery called home. They named him the Father of Route 66 for a reason. First charge of the run.

landmarks.name: Arcadia Round Barn, OK
landmarks.body: A perfectly round barn built in 1898, because someone decided a tornado could not catch a building with no corners. Still standing. Point made.

landmarks.name: Tesla Supercharger - Oklahoma City, OK (W Memorial Rd)
landmarks.body: Halfway across the state and electrons flowing. Big sky, bigger road ahead. Westbound and down.

landmarks.name: Oklahoma Route 66 Museum, Clinton, OK
landmarks.body: The Oklahoma Route 66 Museum, six decades of the Mother Road laid out one dusty decade at a time. This state takes its 66 seriously.

landmarks.name: National Route 66 Museum, Elk City, OK
landmarks.body: The National Route 66 Museum, last big stop before Texas. A whole complex of old buildings frozen mid-century. Oklahoma's parting gift.

landmarks.name: Tesla Supercharger / U-Drop Inn - Shamrock, TX
landmarks.body: Charging behind the U-Drop Inn, a 1936 Art Deco Conoco station so sharp it became Ramone's body shop in Cars. A tower of neon and tailfins. Texas begins.

landmarks.name: Devil's Rope Museum, McLean, TX
landmarks.body: The Devil's Rope Museum, an entire building devoted to barbed wire, the invention that fenced the West. Stranger and better than it sounds.

landmarks.name: Leaning Tower of Texas, Groom, TX
landmarks.body: A water tower leaning hard enough to make you check your tires. Tilted on purpose decades ago to reel travelers in. It still works on you right now.

landmarks.name: Overnight - Amarillo, TX
landmarks.body: Leg one complete. Missouri behind you, the panhandle bedded down for the night. Rest the batteries. Day two runs deep into New Mexico.

landmarks.name: Cadillac Ranch, Amarillo, TX
landmarks.body: Ten Cadillacs planted nose-down in a field since 1974, fins to the sky. Bring spray paint. The dress code here is graffiti.

landmarks.name: Tesla Supercharger - Amarillo, TX
landmarks.body: Morning charge in steak country, home of the 72-ounce challenge. Eat it in an hour and it is free. The car eats electrons instead.

landmarks.name: Midpoint Cafe, Adrian, TX
landmarks.body: 1,139 miles to Chicago. 1,139 miles to Los Angeles. You are standing dead center of Route 66. Every direction is exactly half a road away.

landmarks.name: Glenrio Ghost Town, TX/NM line
landmarks.body: A ghost town split across the state line, emptied out when the interstate skipped it. Boarded windows and dead neon. The road giveth and the road taketh away.

landmarks.name: Tucumcari, NM (Blue Swallow Motel / charge)
landmarks.body: Town of the Blue Swallow Motel, neon since 1939. Old billboards begged drivers for hundreds of miles to stop here for the night. Hard to argue.

landmarks.name: Blue Hole, Santa Rosa, NM
landmarks.body: The Blue Hole, an 80-foot artesian spring in the desert, 62 degrees year round, clear enough to scuba dive. People drive hours for this puddle.

landmarks.name: Tesla Supercharger - Albuquerque, NM (Louisiana Blvd NE)
landmarks.body: Charging just off old Central Avenue, the longest run of surviving 66 neon anywhere. Mountains one side, mesa the other. Mile-high and climbing.

landmarks.name: Tesla Supercharger - Milan, NM (Grants)
landmarks.body: Uranium country just west of Grants. Last easy charge before the high desert and the long climb. Tank up.

landmarks.name: Continental Divide, NM
landmarks.body: 7,275 feet, the roof of Route 66. Rain behind you drains to the Atlantic. Rain ahead runs to the Pacific. You just crossed the spine of the continent.

landmarks.name: El Rancho Hotel, Gallup, NM
landmarks.body: The El Rancho Hotel, where Hollywood bunked while shooting Westerns. Reagan, Hepburn, Bogart. Every room named for a star who slept in it.

landmarks.name: Petrified Forest National Park, AZ
landmarks.body: The only national park Route 66 ever ran through. 200-million-year-old logs turned to stone, and a rusted Studebaker marking the old road's ghost.

landmarks.name: Holbrook, AZ (Wigwam Motel / charge)
landmarks.body: Home of the Wigwam Motel, fifteen concrete teepees with vintage steel parked out front. The Cozy Cone was born here. Sleep in a cone, charge like the future.

landmarks.name: Standin' on the Corner Park, Winslow, AZ
landmarks.body: A street corner the Eagles made famous in 1972. There is a bronze statue, a flatbed Ford, and a steady line of people recreating the album-cover pose.

landmarks.name: Meteor Crater, AZ
landmarks.body: A mile-wide hole punched into Arizona 50,000 years ago by a chunk of space iron. Astronauts trained in it. Worth the short detour off the interstate.

landmarks.name: Overnight - Flagstaff, AZ
landmarks.body: Leg two done. Pines, 7,000 feet, and the address is literally Route 66. Tomorrow you leave the Mother Road and turn south for the desert.

legs.title: Flagstaff, AZ to Maricopa, AZ (leaves Route 66)

landmarks.name: Montezuma Castle National Monument, AZ
landmarks.body: Montezuma Castle, a five-story cliff dwelling tucked into a limestone wall 900 years ago. Wrong emperor, wrong century, great name. Just off I-17.

landmarks.name: Sunset Point Rest Area, AZ
landmarks.body: The best overlook on I-17. The whole Bradshaw range opens up below the rim. Stretch the legs, name the mountains, then drop into the valley.

landmarks.name: Tesla Supercharger - Phoenix, AZ (W Happy Valley Rd)
landmarks.body: Down off the high country and into the Valley of the Sun. Last charge before Maricopa. The thermometer just changed its mind about everything.

landmarks.name: Maricopa, AZ (week-long stay)
landmarks.body: Outbound run complete. Roughly 1,400 miles from home. Mission pauses here for one week. Cameras down, batteries up. The road resumes in seven days.

legs.title: Maricopa, AZ to El Paso, TX (return, not Route 66)

landmarks.name: Picacho Peak State Park, AZ
landmarks.body: That lone spike off I-10 saw the westernmost battle of the Civil War in 1862. Now it is just a very photogenic rock daring you to climb it.

landmarks.name: Tesla Supercharger - Tucson, AZ (W River Rd)
landmarks.body: Saguaro country. The giant cactus grows here and nowhere else in the country. Charging among 40-foot succulents older than your grandparents.

landmarks.name: Mission San Xavier del Bac, Tucson, AZ
landmarks.header: INCOMING TRANSMISSION // SAN XAVIER DEL BAC, AZ
landmarks.body: The White Dove of the Desert, a Spanish mission finished in 1797 and still glowing white against the Sonoran sky. The oldest European building in Arizona.

landmarks.name: The Thing? / Texas Canyon, Dragoon, AZ
landmarks.body: The billboards have been screaming THE THING? for 200 miles across the boulder fields of Texas Canyon. Mystery of the desert. A few bucks to find out. You are stopping.

landmarks.name: Tesla Supercharger - Lordsburg, NM
landmarks.body: Old mining and railroad town, gateway to the ghost town of Shakespeare just down the road. A middle-of-nowhere charge. Embrace the empty.

landmarks.name: Mesilla Plaza, Las Cruces, NM
landmarks.body: Old Mesilla, where Billy the Kid was tried and sentenced to hang. Adobe plaza, Organ Mountains behind it. He escaped, by the way.

landmarks.name: Tesla Supercharger - El Paso, TX (Joe Battle Blvd)
landmarks.body: Back in Texas, hard against the border and the Franklin Mountains. Two countries in one view. Last charge before bed.

landmarks.name: Overnight - El Paso, TX
landmarks.body: Return leg one in the books. The desert southwest crossed in a day. Tomorrow is the long haul across Texas. Pack patience.

legs.title: El Paso, TX to Garland, TX (return, not Route 66)

landmarks.name: Tesla Supercharger - Van Horn, TX (W Broadway St)
landmarks.body: A speck of a town where Blue Origin launches rockets just up the road. Charge here or coast. There is nothing else for a long, long while.

landmarks.name: Tesla Supercharger - Pecos, TX (Pilot Flying J)
landmarks.body: Charging at the Pilot off I-20, in the town that threw the world's first rodeo back in 1883. Home of the famous Pecos cantaloupe and the gunfighter Clay Allison's grave. Electrons and ornery West Texas pride.

landmarks.name: Tesla Supercharger - Odessa, TX (US-385)
landmarks.body: Topping up in the heart of the Permian Basin, pumpjacks nodding to the horizon. Odessa keeps a full-scale Stonehenge replica and the country's second-biggest meteor crater just up the road. Crude underground, electrons overhead.

landmarks.name: Tesla Supercharger - Big Spring, TX
landmarks.body: Charging under Scenic Mountain, where a CCC crew carved a state park into the bluff in the 1930s. The town is named for a real spring that watered buffalo, Comanche, and cattle drives for centuries. Last big charge before the Big Country.

landmarks.name: Tesla Supercharger - Clyde, TX
landmarks.body: Just east of Abilene, edge of the Big Country. The flat is breaking into hills. Halfway across the state and the trees are coming back.

landmarks.name: Tesla Supercharger - Santo, TX
landmarks.body: Last charge before the Metroplex. Palo Pinto hill country, one gas station, and a wall of fast food. Top off and brace for Dallas traffic.

landmarks.name: Tesla Supercharger - Haltom City, TX (Fuel City)
landmarks.body: Last charge of the day at Fuel City on the edge of the Metroplex — tacos, a car wash, and a wall of Superchargers. Fort Worth's blue-collar neighbor, the old Birdville Junction rail stop. One short hop to Garland from here.

landmarks.name: Overnight - Garland, TX
landmarks.body: Return leg two done, Texas nearly conquered. One leg left. Tomorrow the road bends through Arkansas and points home to Springfield.

legs.title: Garland, TX to Springfield, MO (straight home via Paris, TX)

landmarks.name: Eiffel Tower (with cowboy hat), Paris, TX
landmarks.body: Paris, Texas built its own Eiffel Tower in 1993, then bolted a red cowboy hat on top so nobody mixes it up with the French one. About 65 feet of small-town one-upmanship, right on the way home.

landmarks.name: Tesla Supercharger - Henryetta, OK
landmarks.body: Charging at the QuikTrip off I-40, right where the Indian Nation Turnpike dead-ends into the interstate. Old coal-and-glass town; brand-new V4 stalls. Big fill-up before the run across to Arkansas.

landmarks.name: Tesla Supercharger - Lowell, AR
landmarks.body: Last charge of the trip, up in the Ozark foothills north of Fayetteville. This is Walmart and Tyson country, corporate headquarters dropped into the hills. One state to go and we coast home from here.

landmarks.name: Springfield, MO (HOME)
landmarks.body: Home. One big loop closed on the square where Route 66 was born. The Kevin Show signs off. Roll credits.
